    {
      "slide_number": 12,
      "observed_source": {
        "package_part": "ppt/slides/slide12.xml",

      "normalized_workflow": {
        "chosen_title": "MENTAL HEALTH TREATMENT TODAY",
        "title_source": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
        "manual_review_required": false
      },
      "projected_target": {
        "target_package_part": "word/document.xml",
        "projected_slide_heading_text": "Slide 12. MENTAL HEALTH TREATMENT TODAY",
        "projected_slide_heading_style": "Heading1",
        "projected_slide_separator_mode": "PAGE_BREAK",
        "projection_status": "planned"
      },
      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "title",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;140;p17"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;140;p17",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "MENTAL HEALTH TREATMENT TODAY",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;141;p17"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;141;p17",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Asylums have since been replaced with psychiatric hospitals and local community hospitals focused on short-term care.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Emphasis on short-term stays (average stay is less than two weeks).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Due to high costs of psychiatric hospitalization - Insurance coverage often limits length of time individuals can be hospitalized.",
                "level": 1,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Individuals are usually only hospitalized if they are an imminent threat to themselves or others.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Most people are not hospitalized but can still seek psychological treatment.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Involuntary treatment – therapy that is not the individuals choice.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "E.g. weekly counseling sessions might be a condition of parole.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Voluntary treatment – the person chooses to attend therapy to obtain relief from symptoms.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sources of psychological treatment – community mental health centers, private or community practitioners, school counselors, school psychologists or school social workers, group therapy.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Treatment providers include psychologists, psychiatrists, clinical social workers, marriage and family therapists.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": [
        {
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "label": "Visual 1",
            "visual_type": "picture",
            "description": "OSC-Stacked-TM-RGB-1.png",

          "warning_entries": []
        }
      ]
    },

          },
          "normalized_workflow": {

          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "TYPES OF TREATMENT",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {

      "slide_number": 14,

      "projected_target": {

      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",

          },
          "normalized_workflow": {

          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "TYPES OF TREATMENT",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;154;p19"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;154;p19",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychotherapy – psychological treatment that employs various methods to help someone overcome personal problems, or to attain personal growth.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Biologically based treatments or medications can also be used to treat mental disorders.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Biomedical therapy – involves medication and/or medical procedures to treat psychological disorders.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Most often used in combination with psychotherapy.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;157;p19"
          },

            "ordered_position": 3,

            "role_confidence": "low",

                "text": "(Credit: Cadabams Hospitals)",

    {
      "slide_number": 15,
      "observed_source": {
        "package_part": "ppt/slides/slide15.xml",
        "presentationml_root": "p:sld",
        "preview_image_candidates": [
          "slide_015.png"
        ]
      },
      "normalized_workflow": {
        "chosen_title": "PSYCHOANALYSIS",
        "title_source": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
        "manual_review_required": false
      },
      "projected_target": {
        "target_package_part": "word/document.xml",
        "projected_slide_heading_text": "Slide 15. PSYCHOANALYSIS",
        "projected_slide_heading_style": "Heading1",
        "projected_slide_separator_mode": "PAGE_BREAK",
        "projection_status": "planned"
      },
      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "title",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;162;p20"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;162;p20",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "PSYCHOANALYSIS",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;163;p20"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;163;p20",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "First form of psychotherapy, developed by Sigmund Freud in the early 20th century.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Aimed to help uncover repressed feelings.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Techniques:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Free association – patient relaxes and then says whatever comes to mind at the moment.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Freud theorized that the ego would try to block unacceptable urges or painful conflicts during free association causing the patient to demonstrate resistance.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Dream analysis – therapist interprets the underlying meaning of dreams.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Transference – patient transfers all the positive or negative emotions associated with their other relationships to the psychoanalyst.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychoanalysis Today:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychoanalysis is less popular today but Freud’s perspective has been expanded upon by incorporating modern theories and methodology.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychodynamic psychotherapy - Talk therapy based on belief that the unconscious and childhood conflicts impact behavior.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": [
        {
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "label": "Visual 1",
            "visual_type": "picture",
            "description": "OSC-Stacked-TM-RGB-1.png",

      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",

          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;169;p21",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "PSYCHOANALYSIS",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;171;p21"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;171;p21",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "This is the famous couch in Freud’s consulting room. Patients were instructed to lie comfortably on the couch and to face away from Freud in order to feel less inhibited and to help them focus.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Today, a psychotherapy patient is not likely to lie on a couch; instead he is more likely to sit facing the therapist (Prochaska & Norcross, 2010).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;173;p21"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;173;p21",
            "ordered_position": 3,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Figure 16.9 (credit: Robert Huffstutter)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],

        {

          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychoanalytical therapy wherein interaction with toys is used instead of talk; used in child therapy.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Used to help clients prevent/resolve psychosocial difficulties & achieve optimal growth.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Techniques:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Toys, such as dolls, stuffed animals, and sandbox figurines are used to help children play out their hopes, fantasies and traumas.",
                "level": 0,
                "

      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",

          "normalized_workflow": {

          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "WHAT IS PERSONALITY?",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;63;p8"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;63;p8",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Happy, sad, impatient, shy, fearful, curious, helpful. What characteristics describe your personality?",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;66;p8"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;66;p8",
            "ordered_position": 3,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Personality – the long-standing traits and patterns that propel individuals to consistently think, feel, and behave in specific ways.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Comes from the Latin word persona (a mask worn by an actor).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "In ancient times, theatrical masks were used to represent/project a specific personality trait.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },
    {
      "slide_number": 4,
      "observed_source": {
        "package_part": "ppt/slides/slide4.xml",

      "normalized_workflow": {
        "chosen_title": "HISTORICAL PERSPECTIVES",
        "title_source": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
        "manual_review_required": false
      },
      "projected_target": {
        "target_package_part": "word/document.xml",
        "projected_slide_heading_text": "Slide 4. HISTORICAL PERSPECTIVES",
        "projected_slide_heading_style": "Heading1",
        "projected_slide_separator_mode": "PAGE_BREAK",
        "projection_status": "planned"
      },
      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "title",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;71;p9"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;71;p9",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "HISTORICAL PERSPECTIVES",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;72;p9"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;72;p9",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Hippocrates (370 BCE)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Theorized that personality traits and human behaviors are based on four separate temperaments associated with four fluids (“humors”) of the body.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Choleric – yellow bile from the liver.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Melancholic – black bile from the kidneys.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sanguine – red blood from the heart.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Phlegmatic – white phlegm from the lungs.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Galen",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Believed both diseases and personality differences could be explained by imbalances in the humors and that each person exhibits one of the four temperaments.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Prevalent view for over 1000 years and through the Middle Ages.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Choleric – passionate, ambitious, and bold.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Melancholic – reserved, anxious, and unhappy.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sanguine – joyful, eager, and optimistic.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Phlegmatic – calm, reliable, and thoughtful.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },

        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;82;p10"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;82;p10",
            "ordered_position": 3,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Franz Gall (1780)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Proposed that the distances between bumps on the skull reveal a person’s personality traits, character, and mental abilities.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Discredited for lack of empirical support.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Gall developed a chart that depicted which areas of the skull corresponded to particular personality traits or characteristics (Hothersall, 1995).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "An 1825 lithograph depicts Gall examining the skull of a young woman.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },

          "normalized_workflow": {

            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Immanuel Kant (18th century)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Agreed with Galen that individuals could be categorized into one of the four temperaments.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Developed a list of traits to describe the personality of each of the four temperaments.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;90;p11"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;90;p11",
            "ordered_position": 3,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Wilhelm Wundt (19th century)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Suggested that personality could be described using two major axes:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Emotional/non-emotional – separated strong emotions (melancholic, choleric) from the weak emotions (phlegmatic, sanguine).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Changeable/unchangeable – divided the changeable temperaments (choleric, sanguine) from the unchangeable ones (melancholic, phlegmatic).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []

          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "HISTORICAL PERSPECTIVES",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;98;p12"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;98;p12",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychodynamic Perspectives (20th Century)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sigmund Freud:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "First comprehensive theory of personality explaining both normal and abnormal behaviors.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Proposed that unconscious drives influenced by sex, aggression and childhood sexuality influence personality.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Neo-Freudians:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Agreed that childhood experiences matter.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Less emphasis on sex.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Focused on the social environment and effects of culture on personality.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },
    {
      "slide_number": 8,
      "observed_source": {
        "package_part": "ppt/slides/slide8.xml",

        ]
      },
      "normalized_workflow": {
        "chosen_title": "LEVELS OF CONSCIOUSNESS",
        "title_source": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
        "manual_review_required": false
      },
      "projected_target": {
        "target_package_part": "word/document.xml",
        "projected_slide_heading_text": "Slide 8. LEVELS OF CONSCIOUSNESS",
        "projected_slide_heading_style": "Heading1",
        "projected_slide_separator_mode": "PAGE_BREAK",
        "projection_status": "planned"
      },
      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "title",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;111;p14"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;111;p14",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "LEVELS OF CONSCIOUSNESS",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;113;p14"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;113;p14",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Unconscious – mental activity that we are unaware of and are unable to access.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "According to Freud:",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "We are only aware of a small amount (about one-tenth) of our mind’s activities and most of it remains hidden from us in our unconscious.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Unacceptable urges and desires are kept in our unconscious through repression.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "The information in our unconscious affects our behavior, although we are unaware of it.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Freudian slip - Freud suggested that slips of the tongue (saying a word you did not intend to say) are sexual/aggressive urges accidently slipping out of our unconscious.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },
    {
      "slide_number": 9,
      "observed_source": {
        "package_part": "ppt/slides/slide9.xml",

        "preview_image_candidates": [

      },
      "normalized_workflow": {
        "chosen_title": "ID, EGO & SUPEREGO",
        "title_source": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
        "manual_review_required": true
      },
      "projected_target": {
        "target_package_part": "word/document.xml",
        "projected_slide_heading_text": "Slide 9. ID, EGO & SUPEREGO",
        "projected_slide_heading_style": "Heading1",
        "projected_slide_separator_mode": "PAGE_BREAK",
        "projection_status": "planned"
      },
      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "title",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;120;p15"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;120;p15",
            "ordered_position": 1,
            "interpreted_text_role": "title_candidate",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "ID, EGO & SUPEREGO",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;121;p15"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;121;p15",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Freud posited that personality results from efforts to balance two competing forces.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Biological aggressive and pleasure-seeking drives.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Internal (socialized) control over these pleasure-seeking drives.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Freud described this process as an interaction between three systems.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Id",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Contains primitive urges (for hunger, thirst, and sex).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Impulsive, instinctual.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Operates on the ”pleasure principle” – seeks immediate gratification.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Superego",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Develops through interactions with others, learning social rules for right and wrong.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Moral compass that tells us how we should behave based on rules..",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Strives for perfection.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Judges behavior - leads to feelings of pride or guilt.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Ego (self)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Attempts to balance the id with the superego.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Rational",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Operates on the “reality principle” – helps the id satisfy desires in a realistic way.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "The part of the personality seen by others.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },

              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Balanced id and superego → healthy personality.",

    {
      "slide_number": 12,
      "observed_source": {

      "projected_target": {

        {

          "normalized_workflow": {

          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "STAGES OF PSYCHOSEXUAL DEVELOPMENT",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",
            "placeholder_type": "body",
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;146;p18"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;146;p18",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Freud theorized that children pass through five psychosexual stages.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "In each stage, pleasure-seeking urges (id) are focused on a different erogenous zone (part of the body).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Lack of proper nurturing and parenting during conflicts results in the person becoming stuck/fixated in that stage.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Oral Stage (Birth – 1 year)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Erogenous zone – mouth.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Pleasure – from eating and sucking.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Major conflict – being weaned from bottle or breast.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Adult fixation – smoking, overeating, nail biting.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Anal Stage (1-3 years)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Erogenous zone – anus.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {

              "normalized_workflow": {

                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",

              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {

        {

          "normalized_workflow": {

            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Phallic Stage (3-6 years)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Erogenous zone – genitals.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Major conflict – child feels a desire for the opposite-sex parent, and jealousy and hatred toward the same-sex parent.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Oedipus complex (boys) – desire for mother’s attention, urge to replace father. Afraid of being punished by father for these feelings (castration anxiety).",
                "level": 1,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Electra complex (girls) - desire for father’s attention, urge to replace mother. Angry at mother for not providing them with a penis (penis envy).",
                "level": 1,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Adult fixation – vanity, over-ambition.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Latency Stage (6-12 years)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Erogenous zone – none.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sexual feelings are dormant as children focus on school, friendships, hobbies and engage with peers of the same-sex.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Genital Stage (12+)",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Erogenous zone – genitals.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Sexual reawakening – urges are redirected from parents to more socially acceptable partners.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },

                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "placeholder_text",

          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;167;p21",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "body_content",
            "role_confidence": "high",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Individual psychology",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Focuses on our drive to compensate for feelings of inferiority.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Inferiority complex – A person’s feelings that they lack worth and don’t measure up to the standards of others or of society.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Social motives thought to be the force behind thoughts, emotions, and behaviors.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Placed focus on social connections during childhood development.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;170;p21"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;170;p21",
            "ordered_position": 3,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Believed happiness can be found in working together for the betterment of all.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Viewed main goal of psychology to be “to recognize the equal rights and equality of others”.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Saw conscious processes as more important.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Theorized that birth order shapes our personality.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Adler identified three fundamental social tasks all individuals must experience.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Occupational tasks – careers.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Societal tasks – friendship.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",
                  "manual_review_required": true
                }
              ]
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Love tasks – finding an intimate partner.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "number",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": [
                {
                  "warning_code": "NUMBERED_SEQUENCE_REVIEW",
                  "warning_message": "Numbering intent was observed, but ordered sequence should be reviewed in the DOCX.",
                  "warning_scope": "paragraph",

          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychosocial theory of Development",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Personality develops throughout the lifespan.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Emphasizes importance of social relationships at each stage.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Development of a healthy personality and sense of competence depend on successfully completing each of the 8 stages.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }

      "warning_entries": [],
      "text_block_entries": [
        {
          "observed_source": {

              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "CARL JUNG",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",

              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        },
        {
          "observed_source": {
            "source_text_container_type": "standalone_text_box",
            "placeholder_type": null,
            "shape_name": "Google Shape;186;p23"
          },
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "source_name": "Google Shape;186;p23",
            "ordered_position": 2,
            "interpreted_text_role": "unknown",
            "role_confidence": "low",
            "auto_merge_allowed": false
          },
          "warning_entries": [
            {
              "warning_code": "STANDALONE_TEXT_BOX_ROLE_UNCERTAIN",
              "warning_message": "Standalone text box role is uncertain and should be reviewed before merging into surrounding content.",
              "warning_scope": "text_block",
              "manual_review_required": true
            }
          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Analytical Psychology",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Focused on working to balance conscious and unconscious thought.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Carl Jung acknowledged the concept of a personal unconscious but was also interested in exploring the collective unconscious.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Collective unconscious – universal version of personal unconscious, holding mental patterns, or memory traces, which are common to all of us.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Archetypes –patterns that exist in our collective unconscious across cultures/societies.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Represented by universal themes in various cultures reflecting common experiences of people around the world.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Integration of unconscious archetypal aspects of the self seen as part of self-realization process.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Persona – A mask that we consciously adopt.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Derived from conscious experiences and our collective unconscious.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "A compromise between our true self and the self that society expects us to be (hiding parts of the self that do not align with societies expectations).",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "bullet",
                "list_marker_hint": "•"
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "visual_entries": []
    },

          "warning_entries": [],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Extroversion vs Introversion",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Jung’s most important contributions to the field of personality psychology was the idea of extroversion and introversion to explain different attitudes towards life.",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            }

        },
        {
          "observed_source": {

          },
          "normalized_workflow": {

              "warning_scope": "text_block",

          ],
          "paragraph_entries": [
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Energized by being alone",

            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Mental disorder: Significant impairment in psychological functioning, such as schizophrenia or major depression",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Psychopathology: Study of mental disorders",
                "level": 0,
                "list_kind": "plain",
                "list_marker_hint": ""
              },
              "warning_entries": []
            },
            {
              "normalized_workflow": {
                "text": "Statistical abnormality: Scoring very high or low on some dimension",

      "visual_entries": [
        {
          "normalized_workflow": {
            "label": "Visual 1",
            "visual_type": "picture",
            "description": "The x-axis of a chart shows the characteristic level of anxiety. It is divided into 3 parts, Extremely low, Moderate, and Extremely high. The moderate part is shown to extend from low on the left to high on the right. The widths of extremely low and extremely high are the same while the width of moderate is significantly high. The y-axis shows the number of cases. The chart shows a bell curve over the x-axis. It starts from the middle of extremely low part of the x-axis, curves up, reaches its peak at the middle of the moderate part of the x-axis, curves down, and ends at the middle of the extreme high part of the x-axis. The chart shows the note abnormal with a question mark on the extremely low and extremely high parts of the chart.",
            "description_source": "descr",
            "manual_review_required": true
          },

    {
      "slide_number": 3,
      "observed_source": {

        "presentationml_root": "p:sld",
